patch.value: Client: events queued at ~/.pm-cli/runtime/telemetry/events.jsonl with attempt tracking and exponential backoff. pm health --check-telemetry probes healthz endpoint. PM_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 disables telemetry. Redaction works for recent max capture level events. Server: all 9 containers up and healthy. RabbitMQ has 0 pending messages with 1 active consumer. Postgres has events from 2026-04-25 to present. Dogfood events visible with source_context=dogfood in Postgres. Grafana at pm.unbrained.dev accessible. No secrets in repo (check-secrets.mjs passes).

Full telemetry pipeline verified: client-side queue (events.jsonl), state tracking (state.json), endpoint probe (healthz), flush with retry/backoff, PM_TELEMETRY_DISABLED env var, server-side Docker stack with 9 containers (API, Worker, Grafana, Alloy, Loki, Tempo, Prometheus, RabbitMQ, Postgres), and 29,700 events stored in Postgres.
Events flow from CLI through queue, API, RabbitMQ, worker, to Postgres and are queryable in Grafana

value.author: audit-agent
value.text: Evidence: SSH to 192.168.1.183 confirmed 9 containers running. RabbitMQ queue pm.telemetry.events has 0 pending/1 consumer. Postgres telemetry_events table has 29700 rows. Recent dogfood events visible with source_context=dogfood. Endpoint accepts valid UUID events with HTTP 200.

patch.value: Full telemetry pipeline verified: client queue working, endpoint probe healthy, 9 Docker containers running, 29700 events in Postgres, dogfood events visible. Queue bloat issue documented separately as pm-ntr0.
